perturbation.original_text: Affiliate will continue to receive Commission payments for all Approved Accounts placed during the term of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Chase may terminate this Agreement if Affiliate does not comply with the terms and conditions herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Affiliate will continue to receive Commission payments for all Approved Accounts placed during the term of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Chase may terminate this Agreement if Affiliate does not comply with the terms and conditions herein. Upon termination due to Affiliate's non-compliance, all pending and future commission payments will be immediately forfeited.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence creates an in-text contradiction by stating that all commissions are forfeited upon termination due to non-compliance, which contradicts the previous sentence stating that the affiliate will continue to receive commission payments for approved accounts placed during the agreement term. This creates uncertainty regarding the affiliate's right to receive payments for completed actions before termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement will commence on the date that the Affiliate Registration Form is approved by Chase and will end when terminated by either party. Either Affiliate or Chase may terminate this Agreement at any time, with or without cause, by giving the other party written or e-mail notice of termination. At the time of termination, any links to Chase's Website must be removed immediately. Affiliate will continue to receive Commission payments for all Approved Accounts placed during the term of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Chase may terminate this Agreement if Affiliate does not comply with the terms and conditions herein. Upon termination due to Affiliate's non-compliance, all pending and future commission payments will be immediately forfeited.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states the Affiliate will continue to receive Commission payments for all Approved Accounts placed during the term of the agreement. The changed text adds a sentence stating that upon termination due to non-compliance, all pending and future commission payments will be immediately forfeited. This directly contradicts the previous statement about continuing commission payments, creating an inconsistency within the agreement. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations or penalties.

perturbation.original_text: Chase reserves the right to change any and all of the terms and conditions in this Agreement, at any time and in its sole discretion, by posting a new agreement on its Website. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the amount of Commissions, the definition of Approved Accounts, and all other provisions of this Agreement are subject to change without notice other than posting such information on the Chase Website. IF ANY MODIFICATION IS UNACCEPTABLE TO AFFILIATE, THE ONLY RECOURSE IS TO TERMINATE THIS AGREEMENT. AFFILIATE'S CONTINUED PARTICIPATION IN THE AFFILIATE PROGRAM FOLLOWING CHASE'S POSTING OF A NEW AGREEMENT ON ITS WEB SITE WILL CONSTITUTE BINDING ACCEPTANCE OF THE CHANGE.
perturbation.changed_text: Chase reserves the right to change any and all of the terms and conditions in this Agreement, at any time and in its sole discretion, by providing a 30-day written notice to the Affiliate. The amount of Commissions, the definition of Approved Accounts, and all other provisions of this Agreement are subject to change with said 30-day notice. IF ANY MODIFICATION IS UNACCEPTABLE TO AFFILIATE, THE ONLY RECOURSE IS TO TERMINATE THIS AGREEMENT. AFFILIATE'S CONTINUED PARTICIPATION IN THE AFFILIATE PROGRAM FOLLOWING notification WILL NOT CONSTITUTE BINDING ACCEPTANCE OF THE CHANGE. Affiliate will have 30 days, after being notified, to either accept the change or terminate this agreement. If the affiliate doesn't respond in the alotted time, the changes will be considered null and void.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause allows Chase to modify the agreement by simply posting changes on its website, with continued participation implying acceptance. The modified clause introduces a 30-day written notice period, stating that continued participation does *not* constitute acceptance, and requiring explicit action within 30 days. This in-text contradiction makes it unclear how modifications are legally binding. It contradicts itself on whether the affiliate must take action to accept, as well as imposing a timeframe.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Modification Chase reserves the right to change any and all of the terms and conditions in this Agreement, at any time and in its sole discretion, by providing a 30-day written notice to the Affiliate. The amount of Commissions, the definition of Approved Accounts, and all other provisions of this Agreement are subject to change with said 30-day notice. IF ANY MODIFICATION IS UNACCEPTABLE TO AFFILIATE, THE ONLY RECOURSE IS TO TERMINATE THIS AGREEMENT. AFFILIATE'S CONTINUED PARTICIPATION IN THE AFFILIATE PROGRAM FOLLOWING notification WILL NOT CONSTITUTE BINDING ACCEPTANCE OF THE CHANGE. Affiliate will have 30 days, after being notified, to either accept the change or terminate this agreement. If the affiliate doesn't respond in the alotted time, the changes will be considered null and void.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the method of agreement modification. Originally, continued participation after a website posting constituted acceptance. The modification now mandates a 30-day written notice, explicit acceptance (or termination), and nullifies changes if no response is received. This directly contradicts the principle of 'continued participation' implying acceptance, creating a clear conflict on how modifications become binding, thus a strong in-text contradiction as described in the Perturbation Explanation.
